name: Acquired immunodeficiency syndrome (AIDS)
description: Acquired immunodeficiency syndrome (AIDS) — advanced HIV infection with profound immunosuppression.

en.name: Acquired immunodeficiency syndrome (AIDS)
en.shortDescription: Acquired immunodeficiency syndrome (AIDS) — advanced HIV infection with profound immunosuppression.
en.longDescription:
## Definition & Clinical Context

Acquired immunodeficiency syndrome (AIDS) is the advanced stage of HIV infection, defined by profound immunosuppression and the occurrence of opportunistic infections or AIDS-defining illnesses. In intensive care it is relevant both as a cause of admission (opportunistic infection, severe immunosuppression) and as a comorbidity affecting prognosis. AIDS is one of the chronic health items in the APACHE IV severity model.

## Scope

The set captures the documented diagnosis of AIDS and AIDS-related conditions. It represents established AIDS, distinct from asymptomatic HIV infection.

## Mapping Notes

- Map a documented diagnosis of AIDS (or an AIDS-defining condition recorded as such) to this set.
- Asymptomatic HIV infection without progression to AIDS is conceptually distinct; map it only if the intent is to capture AIDS specifically.
